figures.caption: Figure 1: LLMs’ catastrophic forgetting in QA tasks (lower is better). BoolQ’s accuracy drops after fine-tuning on SQuAD and AdversarialQA. While all methods suffer from forgetting across different LLM backbones, our approach dominantly exhibits the smallest performance degradation.
Table 1: Final average accuracy across continual learning rounds on QA benchmarks. Bold denotes the best method, or those within a small effect size of it (Cohen’s d < 0.5).
Table 4: LLM used in our experiments. All models are publicly available on Hugging Face.
Table 5: Core training hyperparameters used across experiments.

Table 2: Final average accuracy across continual learning rounds on text classification benchmarks. Results are reported as mean ± standard deviation over 3 runs. Avg (AA) denotes the mean accuracy across the three curricula. Bold denotes the best method, or all methods within a small effect size of it (Cohen’s d < 0.5).

Figure 2: Overview of ProCL. Given an input x, the model queries relevant parameter slots in the Program Memory W . Through attention, selected programs are composed into an input-conditioned weight Ŵ (x), which is combined with the base weight for execution to produce the output y. The resulting execution weight is then distilled back into the memory via consolidation. This process is conceptually inspired by Complementary Learning Systems (CLS).
Figure 3: Left: Hyperparameter sensitivity of ProCL on QA benchmark. Right: Average runtime (ms per iteration) comparison of ProCL and DEAL across LLMs
